dialogueChunks.intro: More bad news, {player_name}. It never stops.
dialogueChunks.plotStep: The aftershocks have opened a second fissure near {location}, and the ground beneath the emergency shelters is cracking. {target_npc} is evacuating families to the northern ridge, but the ridge is exposed and the {enemy_type} have been circling it all day. We need you to clear the ridge and gather more {quest_item} from the collapsed structures {location_hint}. Without building materials, we can't make the shelters safe for another night.
dialogueChunks.outro: The ground won't stop shaking, and neither will the people. Hold us together a little longer, {player_name}.

playerResponses.text: I'll secure the ridge and bring back whatever I can salvage.
Crumbling ground and circling predators. Your settlement has the worst luck I've ever seen.
If the ground itself is giving way, no amount of supplies will help.

dialogueChunks.intro: {player_name}, the relief supplies you brought? They weren't enough. Not even close.
dialogueChunks.plotStep: We underestimated the damage. The fire weakened the support beams throughout the settlement, and two more buildings collapsed overnight. Nobody was killed, but the medical supplies are buried in the rubble. {target_npc} found an alternative source of {quest_item} in an old storehouse {location_hint}, but it's been sealed for years and the {enemy_type} have nested around the entrance. We need those supplies dug out and the area cleared.
dialogueChunks.outro: I keep thinking this is the worst of it, and then the next hour comes.

playerResponses.text: We're not done yet. Show me where the storehouse is.
More collapsed buildings and more monsters. This disaster has layers.
At some point, you need to accept that {location} can't be saved.

dialogueChunks.intro: The situation has deteriorated, {player_name}. I won't sugarcoat it.
dialogueChunks.plotStep: The floodwater receded, but it left something behind: a thick mud that's contaminated the remaining wells. People are getting sick from drinking the only water available. {target_npc} says there's a clean spring {location_hint}, but the path is blocked by debris and overrun with {enemy_type} displaced by the flooding. We need clean water and we need {quest_item} to purify what's left in the settlement. Without it, disease will finish what the flood started.
dialogueChunks.outro: The disaster didn't end when the water stopped rising. It changed shape. And it's still killing us.

playerResponses.text: Contaminated water on top of everything else? I'll find the spring.
Disease, debris, and monsters. A complete nightmare. Let's deal with it.
I'm not a healer or an engineer. This problem is bigger than me.

dialogueChunks.intro: I thought the worst was over. I was wrong, {player_name}.
dialogueChunks.plotStep: A group of refugees arrived from a neighboring settlement that was hit even harder by the storm. They brought nothing but the clothes on their backs and mouths that need feeding. {target_npc} couldn't turn them away, but now we have twice the people and half the supplies. Worse, the refugees say the {enemy_type} followed them here: a whole pack, tracking them by scent. We need more {quest_item} from the trading post {location_hint}, and we need the {enemy_type} driven off before they attack the overcrowded camp.
dialogueChunks.outro: Too many people, too little of everything, and predators at the gates. We need a miracle, and you're it.

playerResponses.text: Refugees and predators at the door? I'll handle the threat and bring back supplies.
One disaster feeding another. Fine, I'll sort out the immediate dangers.
There are limits to what one person can do, and I've reached mine.
